Spheramid System™
The Maker in the Boardroom
If you want to understand Russ,
look at his hands.
Custom bass guitar
Motorcycle racing
Handcrafted leather bag
Hand-forged cleaver

Russ Laggan forges custom steel knives in his workshop, builds bass guitars from raw wood, races motorcycles, flies a WWII-era P-51 Mustang, and competes in triathlons. He knows that if you overheat the steel, it becomes brittle. If you rush the sanding, the finish never shines. He brings this same craftsman's precision to corporate growth.

In real estate, that philosophy produced results nobody expected. A guy who sold concrete countertops walked into the industry in 2005, and within two and a half years was running his first office at age 34. Not because of connections. Because of results. He then led a team of 15 to recruit 1,692 agents in a single year, shattering industry benchmarks.

Today, as VP of Training at eXp Realty, author of Be Followable, and creator of the Spheramid System™, Russ helps leaders escape what he calls the "death loop" of business, where success leads to burnout, by building systems that attract talent rather than chase it.

Russ's Whiteboard
The Three Questions That Change Everything

Every transformation Russ teaches starts at the whiteboard with three stick figures and three simple questions.

1
Where are you now?
Tell the truth to yourself. Face reality with honesty. That's where real change starts.
2
Where do you want to be?
Dream it. Name it. Get specific about the destination before you start walking.
3
How do you get there?
Build the system. Small steps, compounding improvements. That's the Spheramid way.

Every great business transformation starts at the whiteboard. — Russ

The Be Followable Framework
Four Pillars of Followable Leadership

The framework behind the book, the keynotes, and the movement.

01
Mindset & Self-Transformation
Transform yourself first. Tell the truth to yourself. Internal work precedes external leadership.
02
Presence & Communication
Show up with clarity, consistency, and confidence. Your presence leads before your words do.
03
Trust Through Intentional Visibility
Build trust by being deliberately visible. Not performative. Intentional. People follow leaders they can see.
04
Integrity & Culture Building
Lead with integrity. Build a culture of excellence. When values align with actions, people thrive.
The Book
Be Followable

The Leader's Guide to Inspiring Others and Building Lasting Impact

In a world obsessed with "influencing," Russ argues we have the equation backward. We focus on being loud, rather than being worthy. Be Followable is the leader's guide to building the character, consistency, and competence that makes people want to follow you, even when you aren't looking.

"True leadership is when you hire people you trust, build systems that empower them, and remove yourself from being the only answer."

From Milking the Cow to Creating Cash Cows

Russ's signature distinction: stop extracting from relationships and start building them. The book shows you how to make the shift from short-term transactions to sustainable empires, packed with real-world frameworks and powerful questions from two decades of building teams that produce.

Get the Book Explore the Book →
Free Assessment
Discover Your Agent Style

Powered by the Pentatype Communication Assessment, a validated, five-dimensional profiling tool backed by rigorous psychometric research.

The Concierge
Relationship-first agents who build client loyalty through warmth and genuine connection.
The Strategist
Conviction-driven pros who command attention and close with authority.
The Analyst
Data-driven operators who build trust through precision and market expertise.
The Visionary
Discovery-minded innovators who see possibilities others miss.
The Advocate
Purpose-driven agents who build legacy through mentoring, values, and community impact.

Which One Are You?

Take the free 40-question Pentatype Assessment and discover your primary and secondary communication cores. You'll get a personalized PDF report with your full five-dimension profile, sub-type analysis, and coaching insights from Russ.

Signature Keynotes
Bring Russ to Your Stage

High-energy talks built from the trenches, not theory. Russ uses his hands for visual storytelling (no handheld mics).

The Attraction Advantage

Why the Best Recruiters Don't Recruit. They Produce.

Russ debunks the myth of the "silver-tongued recruiter." Learn the "Lighthouse Principle" of personal branding that causes talent to naturally gravitate toward your organization.

Perfect for: Brokerage Owners, Sales Leaders, Recruiters

The Coffee Mug Test

Are You Sabotaging Success by Overflowing Your Capacity?

A visceral live demonstration of why businesses break. Russ shows how leaders pour "more volume" into "limited vessels," creating a mess instead of momentum.

Perfect for: Entrepreneurs, C-Suite, High-Growth Teams

The Jellyfish Method

Decentralized Growth for the Modern Era.

How to build an organization that is fluid, resilient, and capable of massive scale without a rigid, brittle hierarchy. Based on managing decentralized teams at eXp.

Perfect for: Corporate Strategy, Franchise Operators

eXp University
Training That Transforms

The Spheramid System™ meets the Pentatype Communication Assessment, giving agents a scientific edge in self-awareness and team building.

01

Discover Your Archetype

Every agent takes the communication assessment to identify their dominant archetype and understand how their natural wiring shapes selling, leading, and client communication.

02

Leverage Your Strengths

Each archetype has signature strengths and specific growth edges. This module turns self-awareness into a competitive edge you can use Monday morning.

03

Signal Shifting

The science of adjusting your communication style across contexts: client conversations, team leadership, negotiations, and mentoring. All without losing your authenticity.

04

Team Blueprint

For team leaders: use archetype mapping to build balanced, high-performing teams where diverse communication styles become a strategic advantage.
